questions.question: A set which does not contain any element is called:
questions.explanation: A set with no elements is called the empty set, void set, or null set, denoted by ∅ or {}.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The number of subsets of a set containing n elements is:
questions.explanation: The power set of a set with n elements contains 2ⁿ subsets.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A = {1, 2} and B = {3, 4}, then A ∩ B is:
questions.explanation: Since A and B have no common elements, their intersection is the empty set.
questions.subject: Mathematics
